title: medicago turbinata subsp. aculeata
intro: Medicago turbinata subsp. aculeata, also known as a type of purple medick, is a perennial plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to various parts of the world. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The health benefits of Medicago turbinata subsp. aculeata are said to include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and capsules, and are often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
